items.text: Marathon morning in Mumbai — 42 km done before the city woke up. Legs are jelly, heart is full.

images.alt: Runner crossing a city bridge at sunrise

items.text: Western Ghats long run yesterday — 28 km of mist, mud, and zero regrets. Legs learned things flats never teach.

images.alt: Runner on a misty forest trail

items.text: Episode 14 is live — a long-form conversation on creativity, discipline, and building in public.

audio.title: THE GOOD GUY WEED PAYTCH *audio*

thumbnail.alt: Black and white portrait of two podcast hosts

items.text: On-device AI is finally fast enough for real-time translation on mid-range phones. Tested Hindi ↔ Tamil on a ₹25K device — latency under 200 ms.
Three years ago I was afraid to post anything online. Today I shared my first short film with 200 people I actually know. Small wins.

images.alt: Vintage film camera and reels on a table

items.text: Hyderabadi biryani, but make it weeknight-friendly. One pot, 45 minutes, zero compromise on the dum.

images.alt: Steaming pot of biryani with saffron rice
Fresh spices laid out on a wooden board
Finished biryani served in a brass bowl

items.text: Checked into a cliffside retreat in Udaipur. Infinity pool meets 16th-century architecture — this is the kind of contrast I live for.

images.alt: Boutique hotel pool overlooking a historic city

items.text: Spent six hours in a hide waiting for this. A Bengal tiger crossing the river at golden hour — completely worth the mosquito bites.

images.alt: Bengal tiger walking through tall grass

items.text: Deep-sea vents host ecosystems that don't need sunlight at all. New episode drops tonight — we filmed hydrothermal chimneys 2,400 m below the Pacific.

images.alt: Underwater scene with light rays and marine life

items.text: First drive of the new electric sedan — 520 km claimed range, 0–100 in 4.1 s, and a cabin quieter than my apartment. Full review coming Sunday.

images.alt: Modern electric sedan on a coastal road

items.text: Gym day for runners: single-leg RDLs, calf raises, and core. Strong legs survive marathon month.

images.alt: Athlete training with weights in a gym

items.text: Foam roll, ice bath, early lights-out. Recovery isn't lazy — it's where the adaptation happens.

images.alt: Foam roller and recovery gear on a mat

items.text: A shorter episode with a sharper point. Give it a spin.

audio.title: Side Project Show: The case for slower media

items.text: Pre-long-run breakfast: rice, banana, peanut butter, and way too much coffee. Fuel the miles, not the guilt.

images.alt: Healthy breakfast spread with fruit and grains

items.text: 5 AM headlamp crew — six of us, twelve silent kilometres along Marine Drive before the humidity hit.

images.alt: Runners with headlamps on a dark coastal road at dawn

items.text: Lisbon in June: pastel tiles, tram 28, and pasteis de nata at 8 am before the cruise ships arrive. Already planning a return trip.

images.alt: Colorful Lisbon street with tram tracks
Traditional azulejo tile wall in Portugal

items.text: No spoilers, but the third act of *Midnight Expressway* is the best pure cinema I've seen this year. Go in blind if you can.

images.alt: Empty cinema auditorium with red seats and screen glow

items.text: Final whistle and my legs are done. Worth every minute on that pitch.
Ran benchmarks on three LLM APIs. Latency spread was wider than I expected.
Grateful for friends who tell you the truth gently.
First time making mom's recipe without calling her. Close enough.
Episode day! Tap play and let us know what you think.

audio.title: Night Owls Radio: Behind the scenes: episode mix

items.text: Woke up to mist over the lake and silence except birds.
Rescue shelter volunteer day — every dog had a story.
Cave systems this large shouldn't feel peaceful. They do.
Restored a '98 hatchback over two winters. It starts first try now.
Already planning a return trip before this one ends.
New spectrum data from 120 light-years away. Water vapour detected.
A shorter episode with a sharper point. Give it a spin.

audio.title: Code & Coffee: The future of creative work

items.text: Director commentary changed how I watch the third act entirely.
Early swim set before the city wakes up. 2 km in 38 minutes.
Refactored 400 lines into 90. Same behavior, half the cognitive load.
Deleted the draft three times. Posted it anyway.

images.alt: Laptop and handwritten notes on a desk

items.text: Weeknight dal with too much garlic. No regrets.
The kind of lobby that makes you walk slower on purpose.
Episode day! Tap play and let us know what you think.

audio.title: Morning Commute: Designing for calm technology

items.text: First time seeing a snow leopard on camera trap footage from our team.
Migration patterns shifted earlier this year. Climate data tells the story.
EV charging infrastructure in tier-2 cities improved more than headlines suggest.
Got lost on purpose in the old quarter. Best decision of the trip.
Lab results finally replicated. Third time's the charm.
Indie premiere last night. Budget was tiny, heart was enormous.
Recovery ride after yesterday's race. Slow miles still count.
Your CI pipeline is part of your product. Treat it like one.
Not every day needs a highlight reel. Today was enough.
Tried a new sourdough schedule — crust finally cracked the way I wanted.
A shorter episode with a sharper point. Give it a spin.

audio.title: Code & Coffee: Sound design secrets

items.text: Infinity pool at golden hour — camera roll is full.
The macaws at dawn sound like the forest is applauding.
Behind the scenes: six takes to capture the avalanche timelapse.
0–100 in 4.1 s and a cabin quieter than my apartment.

images.alt: Electric sedan on a coastal road

items.text: Sunrise at the temple before the tour buses arrived.
Public lecture on CRISPR was packed. Good questions from teenagers.
Rewatched a 2003 classic. Still holds up frame for frame.
Match day energy is unmatched. Crowd was incredible tonight.
Long-form audio for your commute — now streaming in the feed.

audio.title: Late Night Lab: The future of creative work

items.text: Shipped a feature in production that was just a whiteboard sketch last month.
Started journaling again. Ten minutes a day changes the whole week.

images.alt: Open journal and pen on a cozy desk

items.text: Street food tour after midnight hits different in monsoon season.
Checked in for one night, extended to three. That kind of place.
Adopted a senior cat. He sleeps on my keyboard within an hour.
New episode explores hydrothermal vents 2,400 m below the Pacific.
Drove the coastal highway with the windows down. Range anxiety: zero.
Long-form audio for your commute — now streaming in the feed.

audio.title: The Daily Brief: How we edit four hours into forty minutes

items.text: Train window views through the highlands — no filter needed.
Climate model update: regional predictions got sharper this quarter.
Cinematography so good I forgot to read the subtitles.
PB day. Nothing fancy — just showed up and did the work.
The best debugging tool is still explaining the bug out loud to someone patient.
Called my parents just to hear their voices. Do that more often.

images.alt: Family gathered around a dinner table

items.text: Meal prepped for the week in 90 minutes. Future me says thanks.
Room service at 11 pm after a long travel day. Peak comfort.
Spent an hour watching otters do absolutely nothing. Best documentary ever.
Filmed bioluminescence for the first time. The crew went silent.
Track day taught me how much I still don't know about braking.
